page: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. This instance is configured with 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core. It features 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. Storage capabilities include a bundled 77 GB local disk, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. The CPU cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for cryptographic and integer instruction sets such as AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, ADX, and FMA.

Benchmark evaluations reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2701.48, while Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks register in the average range. Conversely, multi-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth benchmarks show poor to weak results, with small-block cached reads at 140.21 GB/sec and large-block uncached writes at 22.5 GB/sec. Database operations and Redis SET performance are also positioned in the bottom 10%, indicating limitations under high-throughput transactional workloads.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the E2ads_v5 offers a high memory density per vCPU for an entry-level instance size, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing. However, the tradeoff lies in its limited multi-core scaling and lower memory bandwidth, which constrain its utility for heavy compilation, complex physics simulations, or high-volume database operations. This server is best suited for lightweight memory-optimized tasks, basic web serving, single-threaded applications, and small-scale caching or development environments.
description: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 16.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes a 77 GB local disk and is Premium Storage capable. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, though multi-core performance and memory bandwidth remain weak. Database operations, Redis SET throughput, and software compilation benchmarks fall into the bottom 10% to 25% ranges. Qualitatively, this instance offers high memory density for its size, presenting a cost-efficient profile for memory-sensitive but compute-light tasks. It is best suited for lightweight memory-optimized workloads, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and small-scale caching environments where high parallel processing is not required.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v5 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 77 GB local disk, offering strong single-core performance alongside high memory density.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E2ads_v5 is a 2-vCPU virtual machine with an AMD EPYC 7763 CPU, 16 GB RAM, and a 77 GB local disk for memory-optimized workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, high RAM-to-vCPU density, and local storage for light, memory-sensitive workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB RAM
Bundled 77 GB local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core
Best suited for lightweight memory-optimized workloads and single-threaded tasks
